summary: All automated VP steps pass (typecheck, 980/980 unit tests, migration 0017 schema check, P3 dead-event grep clean except for intentional B9 wiring, B5 render-layer grep clean, session-runner build). E2E pre-deploy VP step for P1 (`last_event_ts` populated within 11s on a live session) FAILS in local dev: an agent_sessions row created with the client_session_id flow (sess-<uuid>) never receives a last_event_ts write because the DO's flushLastEventTsToD1 targets `this.ctx.id.toString()` (the 64-hex DO hash from idFromName) instead of the client-supplied session id that is the actual D1 primary key. This is a cross-cutting pattern in session-do.ts (12+ writes use `this.ctx.id.toString()` against `agent_sessions.id`), not specifically a GH#50 regression — but it does make the spec's primary success criterion (D1 last_event_ts populated) invisible in any flow that uses client_session_id. Recommend NOT merging PR#52 until the ID source is resolved OR confirmed to behave differently in production (e.g. if prod takes a different non-client_session_id path).

vp_steps.name: orchestrator test suite
vp_steps.output: Test Files 81 passed (81) / Tests 980 passed (980) / Duration 6.75s

vp_steps.name: use-now tests
vp_steps.expected: RTL test per spec B4 Verify
vp_steps.output: No test files found, exiting with code 1. Implementation (apps/orchestrator/src/lib/use-now.tsx) exists but no use-now.test.tsx. Spec P2 task list does NOT include adding a use-now test; only B4 Verify narrative mentions one. VP command was written as if the test exists.
vp_steps.note: Additional observation: use-now.tsx deviates from spec Gotchas (line 391) — uses Date.now() as both createContext default and useState initializer, whereas spec mandates 0 + Date.now() only inside useEffect to avoid SSR/hydration mismatch under TanStack Start.

vp_steps.expected: all pass, B9 legacy-drop coverage included

vp_steps.expected: clean build with HeartbeatEvent emission removed

vp_steps.name: P3 dead-event grep
vp_steps.output: 4 hits, ALL in apps/orchestrator/src/agents/session-do.ts: L237, L1633, L1675 (JSDoc comments mentioning the legacy events by name), L3877 (`if (type === 'heartbeat' || type === 'session_state_changed')` — B9 tolerant-drop dispatch). These are intentional and required by B9. HeartbeatEvent and SessionStateChangedEvent TYPES are fully removed from packages/shared-types and packages/session-runner. Treating as pass with note — VP's `expected: empty` was too aggressive.

vp_steps.expected: zero hits (all 8 reader sites swapped to deriveStatus)

vp_steps.command: pnpm wrangler d1 migrations apply duraclaw-auth --local
vp_steps.output: 0017 applied; agent_sessions schema now includes last_event_ts INTEGER

vp_steps.name: E2E pre-deploy P1: last_event_ts populated within 11s
vp_steps.command: axi: sign in + start session + send message; wait ~15s; sqlite3 SELECT id,status,last_event_ts FROM agent_sessions ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
vp_steps.classification: code-defect (pre-existing, cross-cutting — surfaced by this VP)
vp_steps.output: New session sess-9187066b-37c8-4a5b-a849-f3a637cba167 created at 2026-04-22T13:53:55Z via UI. Runner PID 3112926 confirmed spawned and dial-back WS connected. SessionDO's local session_meta SQLite has correct state (sdk_session_id=82219318-..., status=idle, duration_ms=7713, num_turns=2, cost=0.2004775), but D1 agent_sessions row still reads status='running', num_turns=NULL, duration_ms=NULL, total_cost_usd=NULL, last_event_ts=NULL 60+ seconds after completion. updated_at == created_at (no UPDATE ever hit). Zero D1 rows with id matching the DO hash 70c05c248f...
vp_steps.root_cause: session-do.ts:1662 uses `const sessionId = this.ctx.id.toString()` (the 64-hex DO hash from idFromName(client_session_id)), but since commit 2af282c `agent_sessions.id` is the client-supplied 'sess-<uuid>' string. UPDATE WHERE id = <hash> matches 0 rows in the client_session_id flow. Same pattern at session-do.ts:1403, 1442, 1463, 1483, 1508, 1546, 1590, 1616, 1662, 1727, 1840, 3013 — all flushes, reads, and status syncs are silent no-ops for client-created sessions.

vp_steps.name: Post-deploy prod VP (SIGKILL runner, observe transition, query prod D1)

non_blocking_findings.area: use-now.tsx hydration safety vs spec Gotchas (L391)
non_blocking_findings.detail: createContext(Date.now()) and useState(()=>Date.now()) will cause SSR/hydration mismatch if any SSR-rendered descendant reads useNow() without a <NowProvider> ancestor. Spec mandates createContext(0) + useState(0) with Date.now() only inside useEffect.

non_blocking_findings.area: spec P1 task 5 naming rename not applied
non_blocking_findings.detail: session-do.ts:233 still reads `LAST_EVENT_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE_MS` and surrounding JSDoc (L1631–1637, L1651) still says 'debounce'. Spec P1 explicitly required renaming to LAST_EVENT_FLUSH_THROTTLE_MS because the implementation is a leading-edge throttle, not a debounce. Not behavioural — cosmetic.

blocking_findings.area: DO → D1 id mismatch; spec B2 flush is a silent no-op for client_session_id-era rows
blocking_findings.detail: flushLastEventTsToD1 at session-do.ts:1662 (and all other agent_sessions UPDATE sites in the DO) uses this.ctx.id.toString() — the 64-hex idFromName hash — as the WHERE clause value, but the D1 primary key for every row inserted via the optimistic client_session_id flow (commit 2af282c) is the client-supplied 'sess-<uuid>' string. The two IDs are structurally different and never match. Result: spec B2's primary success criterion (D1 last_event_ts populated within 11s, broadcastSessionRow fanout carries the new ts) fails silently. Empirically reproduced: new session's D1 row still status='running' with last_event_ts=NULL long after the session reached status='idle' in the DO's local session_meta SQLite. All three pre-existing D1 rows (from the last 2 days) also have last_event_ts=NULL and stale status='running', consistent with the mismatch applying fleet-wide. Fix likely requires sourcing sessionId from a canonical state field (this.state.session_id or similar) rather than this.ctx.id.toString() in every agent_sessions write/read in session-do.ts.

environmental_notes: Dev orch running at http://127.0.0.1:43613 (derived port for duraclaw-dev2)
Spawn → runner → dial-back end-to-end confirmed working (session_meta populated correctly)
recommendation: Do not merge PR#52 until the DO → D1 id source is reconciled. The code change in PR#52 is internally correct (pure unit-tested predicate + migration + reader swap) but the E2E outcome it promises (D1 last_event_ts populated, client TTL derivation flipping stuck sessions to Idle) cannot be observed for any session created through the current UI flow. Either (a) fix the ID source across session-do.ts agent_sessions writes, or (b) if prod genuinely bypasses client_session_id and uses newUniqueId() (need confirmation), amend the spec VP to reflect that path and add a regression test pinning the invariant so it doesn't silently break later.
